DISCOVERY 610, NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES AND APPEARANCE AT DEPOSITION IS DENIED AS MOOT IN LIGHT OF THE RESPONSES SERVED ON FEBRUARY 11, 2005. COUNSEL FOR BOTH PARTIES FAILED TO MAKE A REASONABLE AND GOOD FAITH ATTEMPT TO INFORMALLY RESOLVE THE MATTERS IN DISPUTE. THE COURT MAKES NO FINDINGS AS TO THE SUFFICIENCY OF THE RESPONSES SERVED AFTER THE FILING OF THE INSTANT MOTION. HOWEVER, THE COURT NOTES THAT SPECIAL INTERROGATORY 	, AS DRAFTED, MAY SEEK PRODUCTION OF MATERIAL PROTECTED BY THE ATTORNEY WORK-PRODUCT PRIVILEGE. THE COURT ALSO NOTES THAT IT IS GENERALLY IMPROPER TO INSTRUCT A WITNESS NOT TO ANSWER A QUESTION DURING DEPOSITION BASED ON AN OBJECTION OF RELEVANCY. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER AS TO WHETHER SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES TO WRITTEN DISCOVERY ARE APPROPRIATE. ALL REQUESTS FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
